vsairshipmod:itemdesc-aerostatica: The refined recollection of researched technologies regarding Aerostats!
vsairshipmod:itemdesc-componentscroll-ti: A secretive scroll revealing the final clue to building a tier1 Airraft!
A secretive scroll revealing the final clue to building a tier2 Airboat!
vsairshipmod:itemdesc-researchpaperitem: The books you transcribed were read one last time before crumbling into illegible dust. fortunately you were able to parse some useful clues and mentions of some interesting research.
vsairshipmod:itemdesc-schematic-tier1: A schematic describing how to construct a tier1 Airraft!
vsairshipmod:itemdesc-schematic-tier2: A schematic describing how to construct a tier2 Airboat!
vsairshipmod:itemdesc-strangecloth: A curious pile of scrap cloth. it seems to be airtight, hydrophobic and flame resistant!
vsairshipmod:itemdesc-airshipmotorshaft: The first of two clockwork-motor components.
vsairshipmod:itemdesc-airshipmotorbrace: The second of two ckockwork-motor components.
vsairshipmod:itemdesc-coatingmix: Thick and sticky, mixing this with water will create balloon coating liquid.
vsairshipmod:itemdesc-airshipfat: Animal fat that has been chopped into smaller chunks for use as burner fuel in a Tier 1 Airraft
vsairshipmod:itemdesc-airshipdeed: This unamusing document describes the proprietary ownership and proper certification of flying safe and responsi-blah blah blah, Oh wow! there's also a spare copy just in case. (only meant for tier 2 and above.)
vsairshipmod:itemdesc-airshipchest-*: There's notches carved on the bottom to help secure it onto an airship
vsairshipmod:blockdesc-figurehead-*: A smaller and lighter replica that fits on airships
vsairshipmod:itemdesc-*airship-tier1-*: It's pretty heavy regardless of how much fuel is still in there.'
vsairshipmod:item-handbooktext-vsairship:
Shipyards are placed on flat ground as a construction zone and launch site of an air-raft. Use the toolmodeselect key to open a small menu to change the orientation of the preview box. Pay attention to the dimensions included in its name and the placement preview. The airship will launch in the direction of the orientation you pick!

(You will be standing by the T1 shipyard's front side when placing it down.)
vsairshipmod:item-handbooktext-*airship-:
How to Construct

Air-rafts are built in a designated construction yard on flat land and will launch where they are built from. Once launched, you can carry the air-raft like a backpack by holding shift and clicking it with rightmouse. (You will not be able to pack it up if it has attachments.) They can be deployed by holding ctrl and scrolling to the backpack slot, then clicking rightmouse on the ground. The air-raft can also be placed as ground storage. Hold shift and click rightmouse to place it on the ground. It can be scrapped for a partial return of materials by holding rightmouse with a saw in your hand. See the Building an Air-Raft entry for more information about construction.

Movement

Once you have launched your air-raft, click rightmouse on the top of the fuelbarrel to sit on the ''seat''. The air-raft can only fly if it has a Temporal Gear and has been fueled with either Rot and / or chopped fat. Holding [Spacebar] fires the burner to move the air-raft up; if you stop burning, the air-raft will move down. Alternatively, you can also hold the ctrl key to open the topside venting hatch to move down right away. When you are in the air, you can use the normal navigation keys to move. The fastest way to travel long distances is to gain altitude and ride the wind currents!

The following rules apply for the wind currents:
1. The precipitation of an area affects the direction of the wind in that area.
2. The weather determines how windy it is for you to ride the currents.
3. Your distance from the ground determines how strong the wind pushes you.

To clarify, if the current weather is only a “slight breeze”, you will only be pushed at the top speed of a slight breeze at max altitude.

VISUAL INDICATION

The weather vanes at your sides show which way the wind is blowing at your current altitude.

The gauge on the barrel indicates the remaining fuel.

The glass gauge behind you indicates the remaining mileage left in the Temporal Gear in use.

TOGGLE HOVER

Above your head is a valve that allows you to turn on a hover mode which passively burns a small amount of extra fuel. It also stops the wind from affecting you. This feature is specifically for use as a lore-friendly flying utility in building vertical structures. It can also be used as a means to recover yourself when the wind overpowers you into a corner that you can't get out of. This feature is limited only to the Tier 1 air-raft.

Equipping

Accessories are added via ctrl + rightmouse.

You can place a shield on the front of the fuel barrel.

You can attach a lantern to the metal hoop attached to the balloon above you.
game:vsairshipmod-tier1-buildinfo-title: Mod Mechanic: Building a Tier 1 Air-Raft
game:vsairshipmod-tier1-buildinfo-text:
Air-Raft Construction

Constructing a Tier 1 air-raft requires a great deal of materials and many steps, but it all starts with a Tier 1 air-raft yard kit (14x7).

Once you've transcribed enough airship research for crafting the aerostatica, use it to craft an air-raft schematic (Tier 1). Use this schematic to craft the shipyard kit.

If you hold the shipyard kit in your hotbar, an in-world schematic box of 7 blocks wide by 14 blocks long will appear in front of you. Place the yard kit on a flat area and make sure to face the shipyard in the direction you want the air-raft to launch in. You can change the direction the shipyard faces by using the [F] key. Once placed down, you will be standing by the front side of the yard.

Afterwards, if you have block interaction help turned on (toggle with blockinteractionhelp), looking at the shipyard lectern will show you the next steps. You will need logs and boards of the same wood type.

For a Tier 1 air-raft's balloon, you will need to gather enough flax twine to produce a visually calculated total of 100 units of plain cloth. Make sure to prepare a flax farm greenhouse with the right fertilizer!

(We have also made a standalone mod which adds multiplied crafting outputs in windmill driven crafting machines to help with this high demand of cloth. This mod is called Clothier's Heirlooms.)

All plain cloth will need to be treated with balloon coating liquid.

The mod config will allow you to set the amount of balloon coating liquid consumed per sealing, which allows you to change the eventual total amount of required items needed to craft enough balloon coating mixture for the coating process. For more information and detailed explanation about this, please visit the modDB page description and look for COATING LIQUID GRIND CONFIGURATION. Thank you!

See the Tier 1 air-raft for more information on how to equip your new air-raft.

You can carry the air-raft like a backpack by holding shift and clicking rightmouse on it. (You will not be able to pack it up if it has attachments.)

As a backpack, it can be unpacked and deployed by holding ctrl and scrolling to the backpack slot, then clicking rightmouse to place it on the ground.

The air-raft can also be placed as ground storage. Hold shift and click rightmouse to place it on the ground.

It can be scrapped for a partial return of materials by holding rightmouse with a saw in your hand.

vsairshipmod:item-handbooktext-vsairship:
Shipyards are placed on flat ground as a construction zone and launch site of an air-boat. Use the toolmodeselect key to open a small menu to change the orientation of the preview box. Pay attention to the dimensions included in its name and the placement preview. The airship will launch in the direction of the orientation you pick!

(You will be standing by the T2 shipyard's right side when placing it down.)
game:vsairshipmod-tier2-buildinfo-title: Mod Mechanic: Building a Tier 2 Air-Boat
game:vsairshipmod-tier2-buildinfo-text:
Air-Boat Construction

Constructing a Tier 2 air-boat requires a great deal of materials and many steps, but it all starts with a Tier 2 air-boat yard kit (16x11).

Once you've transcribed enough airship research for crafting the aerostatica, use it to craft an air-boat schematic (Tier 2). Use this schematic to craft the shipyard kit. If you hold the shipyard kit in your hotbar, an in-world schematic box of 16 blocks wide by 11 blocks long will appear in front of you. Place the yard kit on a flat area and make sure to face the shipyard in the direction you want the air-boat to launch in. You can change the direction the shipyard faces by using the toolmodeselect key. Once placed down, you will be standing by the right side of the yard.

Afterwards, if you have block interaction help turned on (toggle with blockinteractionhelp), looking at the shipyard lectern will show you the next steps.

You will need logs and boards of the same wood type. For a Tier 2 air-boat's balloon, you will need to gather enough flax twine to produce a visually calculated total of 224 units of plain cloth.

Make sure to prepare a flax farm greenhouse with the right fertilizer!

(We have made a standalone mod which adds multiplied crafting outputs in windmill driven crafting machines to help with this high demand of cloth. This mod is called Clothier's Heirlooms.)

All cloth will need to be treated with balloon coating liquid. The mod config will allow you to set the amount of balloon coating liquid consumed per sealing, which allows you to change the eventual total amount of required items needed to craft enough balloon coating mixture for the coating process.

For more information and detailed explanation about this, please visit the modDB page description and look for COATING LIQUID GRIND CONFIGURATION. Thank you!

See the Tier 2 air-boat for more information on how to equip your new air-boat.

It can be scrapped for a partial return of materials by holding rightmouse with a saw in your hand.
vsairshipmod:item-handbooktext-vsairship:
How to Construct

Air-boats are built in a designated construction yard on flat land and will launch where they are built from. They cannot be picked up. It can be scrapped for a partial return of materials by holding rightmouse with a saw in your hand. See the Building an Air-Boat entry for more information about construction.

Movement

Once you have constructed your air-boat, click rightmouse on the starboard aft seat (the seat surrounded by levers) to sit in that seat: this seat can control the air-boat. The air-boat can only fly if it is fueled by (ordered from worst fuel to best fuel) charcoal, anthracite, black coal, or brown coal.
The clockwork engine only functions if it has at least one Temporal Gear.

Holding [Spacebar] opens the gas valve on the gasifier to move the air-boat up. Holding ctrl opens the topside venting hatch to move the air-boat down. You will forcibly lose altitude when the gasifier runs out of fuel. (Although admittedly unrealistic, this is intentional for gameplay balance.) When you are in the air, you can use the normal navigation keys to move. Alternatively or in case of emergency, you can fly without using the engine by gaining altitude and exclusively riding the wind currents at a slower speed.

The following rules apply for the wind currents:
1. The precipitation of an area affects the direction of the wind in that area.
2. The weather determines how windy it is for you to ride the currents.
3. Your distance from the ground determines how strong the wind pushes you.

To clarify, if the current weather is only a “slight breeze”, you will only be pushed at the top speed of a slight breeze at max altitude.

VISUAL INDICATION

The weather vanes above you show which way the wind is blowing at your current altitude.

The gauge on the barrel indicates the remaining fuel.

The glass gauge behind you indicates the remaining mileage left in the Temporal Gear in use.

Equipping

Accessories are added via ctrl + rightmouse.
An ownership deed attaches to the horizontal mast to claim the air-boat.
A lantern can be attached on the large wooden gear at the bow.
A shield can be attached on each of the four outer ends of the cross beams.
A cooking pot or a crock can be placed on top of the gasifier.
A bedroll or an airship treasure chest can be fitted on the cargo slots lining the mast.
An airship figurehead can be attached on both ends of the mast.
A shovel, axe, pickaxe, or prospecting pick can be hung on the side of the boat.
A vessel or chest can be placed on the two front slots.

vsairshipmod:coalfueldepletedvertical: Insufficient coal: Unable to maintain lift!
vsairshipmod:engineatmax3gears: This engine can only take three gears!
